name: Maya Thompson
background: Maya is a young worker who uses the app often to reflect on her daily emotions and recognize patterns in her mental health journey.
goals: Keep track of how her mood changes over time
Use data from the app to support personal growth or therapy
pain_points: Finds repetitive or shallow questions unhelpful
Needs feedback that actually helps her understand herself
Loses interest if the app feels too generic
context: She prefers structured check-ins and meaningful summaries that help her better understand how she feels across different periods.

name: Jordan Ellis
background: Jordan is a college student with limited financial resources who is looking for accessible mental health support without heavy upfront costs.
goals: Use the app without being forced into a subscription immediately
Access useful features before deciding to pay
Feel that the app offers fair and transparent pricing
pain_points: Gets frustrated when features are locked behind paywalls
Feels misled when key functionality is restricted after onboarding
context: Jordan is willing to consider premium features, but only after seeing real value in the free version.

name: Leila Hassan
background: Leila relies on the app regularly and expects it to function reliably, especially when she is using it during stressful moments.
goals: Use the app without crashes or interruptions
Be able to log in and complete tasks consistently
Access important features like reminders and settings without issues
pain_points: Experiences frustration when login or technical issues block access
Loses trust when features do not work as expected
context: Since she may use the app during emotionally sensitive situations, reliability and smooth functionality are critical.

name: Noah Rivera
background: Noah is cautious about digital privacy and is particularly careful when using apps that involve sensitive personal or mental health data.
goals: Clearly understand how his data is being used
Use an app that feels trustworthy and transparent
pain_points: Feels uncomfortable with mandatory consent requirements
Loses trust when the app or its partners seem unreliable or exploitative
context: Noah evaluates apps based on how well they respect privacy, transparency, and ethical handling of user data.

name: Sofia Bennett
background: Sofia is dealing with ongoing emotional challenges and uses the app as a form of support during difficult periods.
goals: Find guidance when experiencing anxiety, stress, or emotional distress
Use the app to better manage overwhelming thoughts and emotions
pain_points: Can feel overwhelmed during moments of high emotional distress
Needs support that is immediate and helpful
Finds shallow or generic advice ineffective
context: Sofia may turn to the app during vulnerable moments, so she needs it to provide calm, reliable, and meaningful support.
